what: Optimize NUM_KV_SPLITS per shape for fp8 path using same heuristic as bf16, and use non-persistent mode (mla_decode_fwd) which avoids separate reduce step for num_kv_splits=1
Use non-persistent mode (mla_decode_fwd) with auto split selection, skip reduce for splits=1+fp8
Use CUDA graphs to eliminate kernel launch overhead between stage1 and reduce
Use fp8 with CUDA graph for all shapes, remove bf16 special case
Fix CUDA graph correctness: include Q quantization inside graph, pre-allocate fixed buffers
Fix correctness: pre-allocate fp8 Q buffers, quantize outside graph, graph uses fixed pointers
Keep CUDA graphs but remove Q fp8 caching - quantize fresh each graph capture
Remove CUDA graphs and Q quantization caching to fix LB correctness. Keep fp8-all-shapes and non-persistent/persistent split logic.
Call mla_decode_stage1_asm_fwd directly for all shapes, pre-allocate all buffers, skip reduce for splits=1
Direct ASM kernel calls with fully pre-allocated buffers for non-persistent fp8 path, bypassing mla_decode_fwd Python overhead
